title: no_main Windows example depends on a magic STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE constant
body: no_main/win/src/main.rs hardcodes `pub const STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE: u32 = 4294967285;`, which is just -11 reinterpreted as u32. It works but it's not obvious why, and it's fragile if someone copies just this snippet without the comment explaining the wraparound. Could this use a named cast expression instead?

title: No example combining no_std and no_main together on Windows
body: The repo has no_std/win/src/main.rs and no_main/win/src/main.rs as separate examples, but readers following along step by step (as the README's technique list implies) hit missing pieces trying to combine both attributes in one binary for the smallest possible Windows executable.

title: build_std example is missing the required Cargo.toml/config.toml snippet
body: build_std/src/main.rs is just a plain 'Hello, world!' -- it doesn't show the `-Z build-std` cargo config or the target spec needed to actually exercise the build-std size-reduction technique the section is supposed to demonstrate.

title: Request a no_std example targeting macOS
body: no_std/nix/src/main.rs and no_std/win/src/main.rs cover Linux and Windows, but there's no macOS-specific no_std example, and libc's printf-based approach in no_std/nix doesn't link cleanly on Darwin targets in my testing.
